title: linum viscosum f. sylvestre
intro: Linum viscosum f. sylvestre, also known as sticky flax or woodland flax, is a perennial plant in the Linaceae family, native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high levels of mucilage, a type of soluble fiber. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting skin health. Herbal preparations of Linum viscosum f. sylvestre can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems.
